title: calochortus clavatus var. pallidus
intro: Calochortus clavatus var. pallidus, also known as the pallid mariposa lily or white mariposa lily, is a rare and endangered species of flowering plant native to the southwestern United States. The plant is used in herbal medicine for its antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties. Traditionally, the roots of Calochortus clavatus var. pallidus have been used to treat various health issues, including fever, sore throats, and digestive problems. Herbal preparations of this plant include tea, tinctures, and infusions, which can be made by steeping the dried roots in hot water or a solvent like ethanol or glycerin.
